_comment: Dispatch rules used by Sisyphus at Phase 0 (Intent Gate) to route tasks to categories/skills. Path: ~/.config/opencode/dispatch-rules.json. Sisyphus reads this via the read tool at intent-gate time to translate task shape into task(category=..., load_skills=[...], ...). Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins. No match → fall through to 'default'.

rules.when: trivial single-file edit, typo fix, simple config change
rules.why: Quick category is pinned to cheap models; no skill needed for trivial work.

rules.when: find code in repo, multiple angles, unknown locations
rules.why: Explore is free contextual grep; parallel beats sequential grep.

rules.when: external library or unfamiliar API, 'how do I use X' for known X
rules.why: Librarian searches external docs / OSS examples via GitHub + Context7 + Web.

rules.when: hard logic, architecture decision, multi-system tradeoffs, threat model
rules.why: Oracle is expensive high-IQ reasoning specialist.

rules.when: ambiguous request, unclear what the user wants, hidden intentions
rules.why: Metis is pre-planning consultant for scope clarification.

rules.when: review a workplan or requirements doc for clarity / verifiability / completeness
rules.why: Momus is the expert plan critic.

rules.when: frontend UI/UX, styling, layout, animations, frontend component work
rules.why: Visual-engineering category runs on a model optimized for visual work.

rules.when: complex creative problem, unconventional patterns, design that breaks templates
rules.why: Artistry category is optimized for non-standard solutions.

rules.when: hard logic-heavy implementation, subtle algorithm, deep inference
rules.why: Ultrabrain is pinned to a model tuned for hard logic.

rules.when: deep research, end-to-end autonomous problem solving, unclear scope hairy
rules.why: Deep category is goal-oriented research.

rules.when: documentation, prose, technical writing, README
rules.why: Writing category is optimized for verbose output.

rules.when: high-effort task that doesn't fit any specific category
rules.why: Generic high-effort bucket.

rules.when: low-effort task that doesn't fit any specific category
rules.why: Generic low-effort bucket.

rules.when: code review before creating a PR
rules.why: CE code review skill provides structured multi-persona review.

rules.when: debug error, trace root cause, fix bug, reproduce from issue tracker
rules.why: CE debug skill provides systematic root-cause analysis with test-first fixes.

rules.when: plan complex multi-step feature, software architecture work
rules.why: CE plan produces durable plan document in docs/plans/.

rules.when: brainstorm vague feature request, product scope unclear
rules.why: CE brainstorm produces requirements doc that feeds ce-plan.

rules.when: infrastructure / selfhost ~/docker/ services — godoxy, crowdsec, pocket-id, etc.
rules.why: Selfhost skill is the canonical runbook for the user's docker stack.

rules.when: dotfiles management / chezmoi / age encryption / encrypted secrets
rules.why: Dotfiles skill manages chezmoi-managed user config with age encryption.

rules.when: git operations — commits, rebase, squash, blame, bisect, log -S
rules.why: Git-master is the canonical skill for all git operations.

rules.when: pirate media suite — Stremio, AIostreams, Prowlarr, Jackett, etc.
rules.why: Pirate skill is the canonical runbook for ~/docker/pirate/.

rules.when: OCI Vault secret management
rules.why: OCI Vault skill creates / retrieves / updates OCI Vault secrets.

rules.when: X Spaces processing pipeline — transcribe, article, publish
rules.why: XSpace pipeline skill handles the full Space → Beehiiv workflow.

rules.when: Prowlarr SQLite corruption / deserialization errors
rules.why: Prowlarr DB recovery skill handles clone-then-fix workflow.

rules.when: MCP security audit / runtime guard / policy enforcement
rules.why: MCP security skills cover runtime content inspection + policy-as-code.

rules.when: domain modeling / ubiquitous language / ADR
rules.why: Domain-modeling skill sharpens project's domain language.
